connectorUiConfig.title: Cisco Duo Security – Telephony Logs (CCF)
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: Ingests **telephony** logs from the [Cisco Duo Admin API v2](https://duo.com/docs/adminapi#overview) directly into Microsoft Sentinel using the Codeless Connector Framework. Signing uses the built-in **CiscoDuo** HMAC API-key authentication (Integration Key + Secret Key) performed inside the polling engine — no signing proxy or Azure Function required.

graphQueries.baseQuery: {{graphQueriesTableName}} | where TimeGenerated > ago(14d) | summarize count() by bin(TimeGenerated, 1d)

sampleQueries.description: All telephony events from the last 24 hours

sampleQueries.query: DuoSecurityTelephony_CL | summarize Credits = sum(credits) by telephony_type, context

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: read and write permissions are required.

customs.name: Cisco Duo API Key
customs.description: A Cisco Duo Integration Key and Secret Key with the **Grant read log** permission are required. Create an Admin API application in the Duo Admin Panel. [See documentation](https://duo.com/docs/adminapi#overview).

instructionSteps.title: Step 1 - Obtain Cisco Duo Admin API credentials
instructionSteps.description:
1. Log in to the [Cisco Duo Admin Panel](https://admin.duosecurity.com).
2. Navigate to **Applications** and click **Protect an Application**.
3. Search for **Admin API** and click **Protect**.
4. Copy the **API Hostname**, **Integration Key**, and **Secret Key**.
5. Ensure the application has the **Grant read log** permission enabled.

instructionSteps.title: Step 2 - Connect Cisco Duo to Microsoft Sentinel
instructionSteps.description: Enter your Cisco Duo Admin API credentials below to begin ingesting telephony logs.
